principle: Update the policy by score-function ascent where each step's per-step gradient weight is the empirical CDF rank of the realized rollout's channel-1 cumulative-return value at trajectory time t against a per-t rolling buffer of historical channel-1 cumulatives from trajectories that reached time t (the alive cohort), simultaneously realizing the substrate-degenerate reduction of the bivariate COPDEV/PARGRAD primitive on DST-concave so the panel run produces load-bearing corpus closure evidence on whether the bivariate-rank region was actually unicriterial in disguise.
primitive_name: per-t alive-cohort marginal empirical CDF F_hat_t
primitive_type: F_hat_t : R -> [0,1] indexed by trajectory time t in {0,...,T_max}, evaluated at the realized rollout's channel-1 cumulative M^1_t to produce per-step weight q_t in [0,1]
feedback_signal: channel-1 scalar reward read from info['vector'][0] per step; channel 2 is read but not used in the weight (logged for shadow-comparison only)
novelty_boundary: UNIRANK is structurally a scalar-channel reduction (channel 1 only, w=[1,0]) of the bivariate COPDEV/PARGRAD primitive on DST-concave's deterministic-given-survival channel-2 substrate. The probe acknowledges this overlap with the scalarization disqualifier explicitly. Its load-bearing corpus contribution is closure-semantic: a head-to-head comparison of UNIRANK's logged gradnorm_var (target ~0.4 to match COPDEV run 21) and final hypervolume score (relative to random 194 and to COPDEV/PARGRAD's logged scores) directly tests whether the bivariate primitive's previously-logged empirical-signal was unicriterial in disguise. The unicriterial weight q_t is itself rank-invariant under monotone reparametrization of channel 1 (not a w^T r at the weight level even though channel access is fixed scalarization). Outcome A (UNIRANK reproduces COPDEV's gradnorm_var and score) closes the bivariate-rank region as substrate-degenerate; Outcome B (UNIRANK fails to reproduce) keeps it open; Outcome C (UNIRANK strictly beats random) opens a unicriterial-rank direction. Each outcome contributes load-bearing corpus knowledge that the bivariate region currently lacks.
empirical_claim: On DST-concave at the quick stage within 120s budget, UNIRANK should produce gradnorm_var > 0.1 mean across training (reproducing COPDEV run 21's ~0.426 mechanism-presence signature), mean episode length T > 2 (avoiding the COPDEV-ablation T=1 collapse), and a final hypervolume score that partitions into one of three load-bearing closure outcomes: A (within +/-20 of random 194, confirming substrate-degeneracy), B (notably below 194 or gradnorm_var separation absent, falsifying substrate-degeneracy), or C (strictly above 200, opening a unicriterial-rank direction).
falsifier: Primary mechanism-presence falsifier: gradnorm_var below 0.05 mean and statistically indistinguishable from the uniform-weight ablation throughout training would falsify the rank-weight non-uniformity claim. Closure-determining falsifier: gradnorm_var notably below COPDEV run 21's logged 0.426 (e.g., < 0.15) supports Outcome B, falsifying substrate-degeneracy and keeping the bivariate-rank region open. Substrate-signal falsifier: final score strictly worse than the uniform-weight ablation by more than seed variance falsifies any usefulness claim.
ablation_plan: Primary ablation: replace per-step rank weight q_t with constant q_t = 1, recovering REINFORCE-without-baseline on the gradient-coefficient slot. In train_ablate.py, skip CDF computation and buffer maintenance; in the score-function update use q_t = 1 for all t. Same hyperparameters, policy architecture, rollout mechanism. The ablation removes the per-step rank-weight non-uniformity. Optional sanity ablation: replace q_t with q_t ~ Uniform(0,1) to preserve weight non-uniformity but remove systematic rank correlation; this is logging-only on mean_qt_trend, not the load-bearing comparison.
proof_debt: Convergence of UNIRANK iterates to a stationary point of the modified objective J_tilde(pi) = E_tau[Sigma_t F^pi_t(M^1_t)] under two-timescale Borkar dynamics with the alive-cohort buffer on the slow timescale. Substrate-degeneracy formal characterization: prove the empirical equivalence of UNIRANK's q_t and PARGRAD's p_t up to O(1/N) tie-correction on DST-concave alive cohorts (formalizing run 22 derivation step 5). Selection bias of the alive-cohort estimator F_hat_t under survival conditioning. Conditional corpus closure statement under Outcome A: the bivariate-rank region reduces to the unicriterial-rank region on substrates with deterministic-given-survival channel structure.
